storyAcceptanceCriteria: User can navigate to the cart page
User can see all items added to the cart
User can see item details such as name, quantity, and price in the cart
User can see the total price of items in the cart
User is informed when the cart is empty

testCases.title: View cart with items successfully added
preconditions: User is logged in or browsing as a guest
At least one item has been added to the cart
User is on the homepage or any product page
steps: 1. Locate the cart icon or 'View Cart' link in the navigation header
2. Click on the cart icon or 'View Cart' link
3. Observe the cart page or cart drawer that opens
4. Verify that all previously added items are displayed
5. Verify each item shows the product name
6. Verify each item shows the quantity
7. Verify each item shows the unit price
8. Verify a subtotal or total price is displayed at the bottom of the cart
testCases.expectedResult: The cart page displays all added items with their correct names, quantities, and prices, and the total cost is accurately calculated and visible to the user.
acceptanceCriteria: User can navigate to the cart page
User can see all items added to the cart
User can see item details such as name, quantity, and price in the cart
User can see the total price of items in the cart
testCases.automationReason: This is a stable happy-path UI flow with clearly verifiable DOM elements such as item names, prices, quantities, and totals that Playwright can reliably locate, assert, and validate across browsers.
